skill.name: Azure.AI.Projects (.NET)
skill.description: Azure AI Projects SDK for .NET. High-level client for Azure AI Foundry projects including agents, connections, datasets, deployments, evaluations, and indexes.
skill.summary: Complete SDK guide for Azure AI Foundry project operations with .NET including versioned agents and AI orchestration.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation-only skill providing guidance for Azure AI Projects SDK for .NET. Static analysis found 0 security issues with a risk score of 0/100. The skill contains code examples that follow Azure security best practices including DefaultAzureCredential for authentication and async patterns for I/O operations. No executable code, network calls, or suspicious patterns detected.

content.user_title: Build AI agents with Azure AI Foundry for .NET
content.value_statement: Developers struggle to integrate Azure AI Foundry services into .NET applications. This skill provides complete SDK guidance for agents, connections, datasets, and evaluations with production-ready patterns.
actual_capabilities: Guide creation of persistent agents with code interpreter and file search tools
Explain versioned agent patterns using Azure.AI.Projects.OpenAI preview package
Demonstrate connection, deployment, and dataset management workflows
Provide evaluation configuration examples for AI model assessments
Show Azure AI Search index creation and management patterns
Explain authentication with DefaultAzureCredential for production scenarios
limitations: Requires Azure AI Foundry project and appropriate Azure subscriptions
Some features like versioned agents require preview packages
Does not execute code - provides documentation and guidance only
Code examples target .NET 8+ and Azure SDK beta versions

use_cases.title: Enterprise AI Agent Development
use_cases.description: Build production AI agents with persistent threads, tool integration, and versioned deployments for enterprise applications.

use_cases.title: AI Model Evaluation Pipeline
use_cases.description: Create automated evaluation workflows for AI models using built-in evaluators for relevance, fluency, and groundedness metrics.
use_cases.target_user: ML engineers and data scientists validating AI model performance

use_cases.title: RAG Solution with Azure AI Search
use_cases.description: Implement retrieval-augmented generation solutions connecting AI agents to Azure AI Search indexes for knowledge-based responses.
use_cases.target_user: Solution architects designing knowledge-aware AI systems

prompt_templates.title: Basic Agent Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a simple persistent agent using Azure.AI.Projects SDK. I need an agent that can answer customer support questions using the gpt-4o-mini model. Show me the authentication setup and basic agent creation code.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - Creating your first Azure AI agent

prompt_templates.title: Agent with File Search
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to create an agent that can search through uploaded documentation files. Show me how to configure the FileSearchToolDefinition, upload a dataset, and attach it to a persistent agent in Azure AI Foundry.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate - Adding retrieval capabilities to agents

prompt_templates.title: Versioned Agent Deployment
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain how to use Azure.AI.Projects.OpenAI package to create versioned agents. I need to deploy an agent with web search tools, get the response client, and manage agent versions for production rollout.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced - Production agent versioning and deployment

prompt_templates.title: Evaluation Framework Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up an evaluation pipeline for my AI agent. Show me how to configure the Relevance evaluator, create an input dataset, run the evaluation, and interpret the results using the EvaluationsClient.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced - AI model evaluation and quality assurance

output_examples.input: Create a math tutor agent that can solve equations step by step
output_examples.output: Agent created with ID 'agent-12345'. Instructions set to guide users through equation solving with step-by-step explanations. Thread initialized and ready for user queries.

output_examples.input: Evaluate my agent's responses for relevance and accuracy
output_examples.output: Evaluation job 'eval-67890' completed. Relevance score: 0.87, Accuracy score: 0.92. 15 of 20 test cases passed. Detailed report available in Azure AI Foundry portal.

best_practices: Use DefaultAzureCredential for production authentication to leverage managed identities and avoid hardcoded credentials
Always use async methods for I/O operations and poll with 500ms delays when waiting for agent runs to complete
Clean up resources by deleting threads, agents, and datasets when no longer needed to avoid unnecessary costs
anti_patterns: Do not use includeCredentials: true unless you specifically need credential values - this exposes sensitive information
Avoid synchronous methods in production code as they block threads and reduce scalability
Do not store agent or thread IDs in client-side code without proper validation and access controls

faq.question: What is the difference between AIProjectClient and PersistentAgentsClient?
faq.answer: AIProjectClient is the main entry point for all Azure AI Foundry operations. GetPersistentAgentsClient() returns a specialized client for low-level agent operations including thread management and run execution.

faq.question: Do I need preview packages to use Azure AI Projects SDK?
faq.answer: The core SDK (Azure.AI.Projects) is generally available. Preview packages like Azure.AI.Projects.OpenAI and Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent are only needed for advanced features like versioned agents and low-level operations.

faq.question: How do I authenticate to Azure AI Foundry from my .NET application?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential which automatically tries multiple authentication methods including managed identities, Azure CLI credentials, and environment variables. This is the recommended approach for production deployments.

faq.question: Can I use Azure AI Projects SDK with existing OpenAI code?
faq.answer: Yes, you can get an AzureOpenAIClient from the project client and use standard OpenAI chat completions. The SDK provides GetConnection and GetProjectResponsesClientForAgent methods for OpenAI integration.

faq.question: What tools are available for Azure AI agents?
faq.answer: Available tools include Code Interpreter for Python execution, File Search for document queries, Bing Grounding for web search, Azure AI Search for custom indexes, OpenAPI for external APIs, Azure Functions, and MCP for Model Context Protocol.

faq.question: How do I handle errors when calling Azure AI Foundry APIs?
faq.answer: Wrap API calls in try-catch blocks and handle RequestFailedException. Check the Status code, ErrorCode, and Message properties to determine the appropriate retry or fallback behavior.
